qa_pairs.question: What is the problem with wasted CPU cycles in META-Farm when jobs frequently hit their run-time limit?
qa_pairs.answer: When META-Farm jobs repeatedly hit their run-time limit and abort mid-processing, it leads to wasted CPU cycles. Even though `resubmit.run` can restart the failed or never-ran cases automatically, this inefficiency can be significant. On average, the waste is estimated at "0.5 x N_jobs x average_case_run_time." For instance, if 1000 meta-jobs run with an average case run-time of 1 hour, approximately 500 CPU-hours (or 20 CPU-days) could be wasted.

qa_pairs.question: How do the META-Farm scripts address the issue of wasted CPU cycles caused by jobs hitting their run-time limits?
qa_pairs.answer: The META-Farm scripts incorporate built-in intelligence specifically designed to mitigate the problem of wasted CPU cycles when jobs encounter run-time limits. This mitigation strategy is implemented within the `task.run` script.
